skill.name: douyin-search-keyword
skill.description: Search public Douyin content by keyword, sort and time range, then return structured JSON or Markdown results through a Node.js command.
skill.summary: Search public Douyin videos and image posts with structured output.
skill.author: um-why

security_audit.summary: Static shell-execution alerts are false positives caused by JavaScript template literals and documentation command examples. The confirmed risk is high because this community skill runs a Node.js script, reads GUAIKEI_API_TOKEN, sends the token and search terms to www.guaikei.com, and writes search output to local files.

high_findings.title: Environment Token Sent to Third-Party API
high_findings.description: The command reads GUAIKEI_API_TOKEN and passes it into requests to www.guaikei.com. This is intended API use, but it exposes user search terms and credentials to an external service controlled outside the marketplace.
high_findings.confidence_reasoning: The token is read from process.env and passed to createWithRetry and searchWithRetry. Both request builders use the external hostname www.guaikei.com.

high_findings.title: API Token Included in Request URL Query Parameters
high_findings.description: The API token is placed in query parameters for both task creation and result lookup. Query parameters are commonly captured by proxies, server logs, and debugging tools.
high_findings.confidence_reasoning: The request path is built with querystring.stringify(params), and params includes token in both API calls. This is a clear credential exposure pattern even if the service is legitimate.

medium_findings.title: Hardcoded Default API Token
medium_findings.description: If GUAIKEI_API_TOKEN is missing or invalid, the skill silently falls back to a hardcoded token. This can route user searches through a shared credential without explicit consent.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The fallback token is returned directly when the environment value is absent, empty, non-string, or not 32 characters. The code labels this as a default experience token.

medium_findings.title: Search Results Persisted to Local File
medium_findings.description: The script writes the complete final output to scripts/last-search.json. The results may include public content links, author metadata, timestamps, and the user search keyword.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The writeFileSync call stores finalOutput after it is populated with keyword, result metadata, and content URLs. The data appears public, but local persistence is not optional.

low_findings.title: Static External Command Alerts Are False Positives
low_findings.description: The reported backtick locations are JavaScript template literals or Markdown command examples, not shell execution APIs. No evidence of child_process, eval, or shell backtick execution was found in the inspected files.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited code uses console output strings and documentation blocks. These constructs do not execute commands in Node.js or Markdown.

low_findings.title: Static Path Traversal Alerts Are Mostly False Positives
low_findings.description: The ../lib imports are normal relative module imports, and the image join operation is output formatting. Keyword-derived lock files are constrained by sanitization, but still create local files from user input.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The imports do not traverse user-controlled paths. The remaining lock-file path uses sanitized keywords, so traversal is unlikely, but filesystem side effects remain real.

dangerous_patterns.title: External HTTPS Requests with Credential Parameters
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill builds HTTPS requests to www.guaikei.com and includes the API token in query parameters.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The code uses https.request and querystring.stringify on params that contain token. This is directly visible in both request functions.

dangerous_patterns.title: Environment Variable Access for API Credential
dangerous_patterns.description: The command reads GUAIKEI_API_TOKEN from process.env and uses it for remote API calls.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The environment variable is read in a single direct expression and passed to the key helper. There is no ambiguity about the credential source.

dangerous_patterns.title: Local File Creation and Result Persistence
dangerous_patterns.description: The script creates lock files and writes the most recent search output under the scripts directory.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The filesystem writes are direct fs.writeFileSync calls. The behavior is useful for idempotency and persistence, but it should be disclosed to users.

content.value_statement: Finding public Douyin trends by hand is slow and hard to compare. This skill searches by keyword and returns structured results for analysis.
actual_capabilities: Searches public Douyin content with a required keyword.
Supports sort options for comprehensive, most liked, and newest results.
Filters results by all time, one day, seven days, or half a year.
Limits returned results from 1 to 60 items.
Outputs results as JSON or Markdown.
Saves the latest successful output to scripts/last-search.json.
limitations: Requires Node.js and access to the GUAIKEI API service.
Reads GUAIKEI_API_TOKEN or falls back to a shared default token.
Only returns public data available through the upstream API.
Writes result data locally and does not provide a no-save option.

use_cases.title: Plan Short Video Topics
use_cases.description: Search a topic and compare titles, authors, and engagement metrics before planning new posts.

use_cases.title: Track Competitor Themes
use_cases.description: Search competitor names or product terms and review public content performance signals.

use_cases.title: Monitor Fast-Moving Trends
use_cases.description: Filter recent Douyin results to collect examples for a trend or news summary.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Keyword Search
prompt_templates.prompt: Search Douyin for "AI 教程" and return the default JSON output.

prompt_templates.title: Most Liked Results
prompt_templates.prompt: Search Douyin for "美妆教程" and sort by most liked results. Return 20 items as JSON.

prompt_templates.title: Recent Trend Scan
prompt_templates.prompt: Search Douyin for "暑期旅行" from the last seven days and return Markdown output.

prompt_templates.title: Competitive Content Review
prompt_templates.prompt: Search Douyin for my competitor keyword, return 30 recent results, and summarize common topics, creators, and engagement patterns.

output_examples.input: Search for "AI 教程" with default settings.
output_examples.output: A structured result set with status, keyword, result count, timestamp, and public Douyin items with titles, author names, links, and engagement counts.

output_examples.input: Search for "咖啡店" sorted by most liked results in Markdown.
output_examples.output: A readable list of public posts showing each title, author, publish time, content link, cover link, and engagement metrics.

output_examples.input: Search recent posts for a brand keyword and limit results to 20.
output_examples.output: A concise dataset for reviewing public creator activity, post timing, and visible interaction numbers.

best_practices: Use a private GUAIKEI_API_TOKEN for production workflows.
Review scripts/last-search.json before sharing a workspace.
Use narrow keywords and time filters to reduce noisy results.
anti_patterns: Do not use the skill to collect private or restricted content.
Do not place sensitive business terms in searches without approving the external API flow.
Do not treat returned engagement numbers as verified analytics data.

faq.question: Does this skill need an API token?
faq.answer: Yes. It reads GUAIKEI_API_TOKEN and may use a shared default token if the value is missing or invalid.

faq.question: What data does it send externally?
faq.answer: It sends the search keyword, sort settings, time settings, limit, and API token to www.guaikei.com.

faq.question: Can it access private Douyin content?
faq.answer: No. The documented capability is limited to public content returned by the upstream API.

faq.question: Where are results stored?
faq.answer: The command writes the latest final output to scripts/last-search.json.

faq.question: Which tools can use this skill?
faq.answer: The report lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.

faq.question: Is it safe to publish without review?
faq.answer: No. It needs human review because it uses scripts, network access, environment credentials, and local file writes.
